channel.title: Trending Tech News - Krynn Tools
channel.description: Latest trending news in technology, AI, and cybersecurity. Curated from trusted sources, updated automatically.

item.title: Noom Promo Codes: 50% Off Best Deals & Free Trials for July 2026
item.description: Discover the best ways to save on Noom subscriptions, including free trials, limited-time offers for GLP-1Rx Plus, and essential tips for redeeming your Noom discount.
item.dc:creator: WIRED

item.description: Save on rowers and accessories with Hydrow coupons, including an exclusive discount of $50.

item.description: Shop the Naturepedic sale this 2026.

item.description: Get 10% off with Wayfair promo code, up to 80% off furniture, and more top coupons.

item.title: 1Password Coupon: Score a Free Trial in July 2026
item.description: Save up to 28% on business and personal memberships with 1Password promo codes and deals.

item.title: HelloFresh Promo Codes: 55% Off for July 2026
item.description: Get up to 55% off and free meal boxes using a HelloFresh coupon code in July 2026. Discover our best codes and discounts to let you save time and money.

item.description: Discover how to save on T-Mobile Business Internet and phone plans, from sign-up perks and switching rewards to bundled offers and free lines.

item.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

item.title: Lorde says AI glasses are not sexy
item.description: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said onstage.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: OpenAIs first hardware device is reportedly a screenless speaker that can move
item.description: The device is weirdly described as involving "mechanical elements that can move on their own" and the Bloomberg report includes the detail that the device is designed to "feel like a companion and become a physical manifestation of OpenAI’s ChatGPT."
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Microsoft’s Secure Boot has been broken for a decade and no one noticed until now
item.description: Microsoft’s Secure Boot has been broken for a decade and no one noticed until now
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: Trump admin puts Americans in Congo on "do-not-board" list, barring return
item.description: Trump admin puts Americans in Congo on "do-not-board" list, barring return
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: The UK Is Planning a Social Media Curfew for 16- and 17-Year-Olds
item.description: The restrictions, which can be turned off, will include a crackdown on “addictive” app features and will be in addition to a total ban on children under 16 accessing platforms like TikTok and YouTube.
item.dc:creator: WIRED

item.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
item.description: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: OpenAIs new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
item.description: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: SonicWall warns of SMA1000 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks, patch now
item.description: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked as CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released security updates. [...]
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Dependabot version updates introduce default package cooldown

item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
item.description: Microsoft shipped its largest Patch Tuesday on record today, and two of the fixes close holes that attackers are already exploiting. The release covers 622 of Microsoft's own CVEs by its Security Update Guide count, more than triple June's previous high of around 200. Those two live b
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Spanish Police take down €140 million cyber fraud ring, arrest four
item.description: The Spanish Police dismantled a cybercrime and money-laundering organization that made €140 million ($160 million) from investment fraud and business email compromise (BEC) attacks. [...]
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
item.description: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: How I use HTMX with Go
item.description: Article URL: https://www.alexedwards.net/blog/how-i-use-htmx-with-go Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48912175 Points: 145 # Comments: 34
item.dc:creator: Hacker News

item.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
item.description: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Anthropics newest ad is creeping people out
item.description: Anthropic has consistently attempted to depict itself as the ethical foil to other AI companies. This latest marketing stunt — which leans into criticism of AI as a way to make Anthropic seem aware of the responsibility it carries — would appear to be more of the same.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
item.description: Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant fixed in its record-smashing Patch Tuesday release last month. Microsoft attributed the burgeoning p
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Nearly 300 GitHub repos pose as legit software to push malware
item.description: A threat actor has published hundreds of fake GitHub repositories impersonating legitimate software and security projects to distribute infostealer malware. [...]
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Probe into explosive diarrheal cases points to Taco Bell and bad lettuce
item.description: Probe into explosive diarrheal cases points to Taco Bell and bad lettuce
item.dc:creator: Ars Technica

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Vulnerabilities, Including Two Exploited Zero-Days
item.description: Two flaws in Active Directory and SharePoint Server have been exploited as zero-days, and a BitLocker bug was publicly disclosed. The post Microsoft Patches Record 622 Vulnerabilities, Including Two Exploited Zero-Days appeared first on SecurityWeek.
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update, which includes the July 2026 Patch Tuesday security updates for 570 vulnerabilities, along with additional security fixes. [...]
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer

item.title: Lucid Motors denies report its considering bankruptcy
item.description: The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
item.description: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch

item.title: Synopsys Finds No Evidence of Data Breach Amid Bosch Hack Claims
item.description: The D1R cybercrime group claimed to have stolen valuable data from Synopsys and Bosch, threatening to leak it unless a ransom is paid. The post Synopsys Finds No Evidence of Data Breach Amid Bosch Hack Claims appeared first on SecurityWeek.
item.dc:creator: SecurityWeek

item.title: SAP Patches CVSS 9.9 NetWeaver ABAP Flaw That Could Expose or Modify Data
item.description: SAP has rolled out updates to address multiple vulnerabilities as part of its July 2026 security updates, including a critical flaw in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-44747 (CVSS score: 9.9), an out-of-bounds write flaw that allows an authenticated at
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft July 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes massive 570 flaws, 3 zero-days
item.description: Today is Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday, and with it comes security updates for a record-breaking 570 flaws, including two zero-day vulnerabilities exploited in attacks and one publicly disclosed. [...]
item.dc:creator: BleepingComputer
